reasoning: The Lean signature captures Baire's theorem exactly as stated informally. The hypotheses `[MetricSpace X] [CompleteSpace X] [Nonempty X]` correspond precisely to "nonempty complete metric space X". The function `G : ℕ → Set X` with `hG : ∀ n, IsOpen (G n) ∧ Dense (G n)` corresponds to "sequence {G_n} of dense open sets". The conclusion `∃ x, ∀ n, x ∈ G n` is definitionally equivalent to `(⋂ n, G n).Nonempty`, which is exactly "⋂_{1}^{∞} G_n is not empty". The 1-indexing vs ℕ-indexing is immaterial since both describe the same countable intersection. No hypotheses are silently added or omitted, and the types match standard mathematical reading.
